channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Pavlov's Waymo: How Self-Driving Cars Are Classically Conditioning Our Pets (and Us)
item.description:
Discover the fascinating connection between Pavlov's classical conditioning and self-driving cars, which are shaping our pets' behavior and our own reactions to daily cues."**
**The Power of Classical Conditioning**

Pavlov's Legacy Lives On in Modern Times

Most of us know the general story behind Ivan Pavlov's groundbreaking discovery. He used a metronome as a stimulus around dogs he was studying, and soon, they would start to salivate. This phenomenon became one of modern psychology's foundational discoveries.

The process is an unconscious one where a neutral stimulus becomes associated with a naturally occurring stimulus, eventually leading to a connection between the two. The dogs would immediately know they were about to have a meal when seeing the researcher who often brings them food or hearing the noise of the cart on its way.

**Riding in Self-Driving Cars: A Familiar Experience**

My Dog's Unconscious Learning Process

Flash forward 120 years, and my dog and I are riding through San Francisco in a self-driving car. My chiweenie, Poppy, usually naps peacefully, facing inward, oblivious to the world outside.

Near the end of each ride, the car makes a familiar "ding-dong" chime, followed by a woman's voice reminding me to take my phone, keys, and wallet. Poppy would remain in a deep sleep until the car stopped, I unbuckled my seatbelt, and picked her up to get out.

**Pavlov's Waymo? The Connection Revealed**

Classical Conditioning in Action

Lately, I've noticed something strange: As soon as the ding sounds, Poppy wakes up, turns around, and readies herself at the door without my help—every single time.

I emailed the veterinary team at Bond Vet to ask about this phenomenon. The short answer? Yes, it's Pavlov's Waymo.

**The Science Behind It**

Dogs' Incredible Perception and Learning Capabilities

“In practical terms, the sound acts as a cue, prompting her anticipation to leave. This behavior develops because the sound repeatedly coincides with the end of the ride, and the reward of getting out reinforces her response,”

Dr. Lisa Lippman, director of virtual medicine at Bond Vet, explained in an email. “Dogs are incredibly perceptive and often pick up on routines and environmental cues like this, it’s a great example of how they learn and adapt!”

**The Connection to Human Experience**

Classical Conditioning in Our Daily Lives

Roughly around the same time, researchers at the University of California, Davis, found that common household noises can cause a dog anxiety. Thankfully, Poppy doesn't seem anxious about the car's noise.

This made me think of our world where constant notifications and dings condition us to associate sounds with emotions. The microwave beep alerts us that we're about to be rewarded with food, the "tudum" sound when you open the Netflix app prepares us for entertainment.

**Brands Utilizing Classical Conditioning**

Marketing Strategies Exploiting Human Psychology

“When we play sound feedback for Waymo riders, our guiding philosophy is to be friendly and helpful,”

Waymo's Head of Design and Customer Research Ryan Powell said over email. “That means playing sounds that feel connected and familiar, but not intrusive. We want to be thoughtful about how and when we play sound, so that riders can rely on these signals for their safety and comfort.

**Conclusion: The Power of Classical Conditioning**

A Lasting Legacy in Modern Times

The self-driving cars are classically conditioning our pets' behavior. But they also condition us as humans, shaping our reactions to daily cues and associating sounds with emotions.

As we continue to navigate this changing world, it's essential to recognize the power of classical conditioning and its far-reaching implications on our lives.
item.dc:creator: Major Digest
